# Compatibility Report
- Name of the game with compatibility issues: SONIC X SHADOW GENERATIONS
- Steam AppID of the game: 2513280

## System Information
- GPU: GTX 980 Ti
- Video driver version: nvidia 570.169
- Kernel version: 6.8.0-78-generic
- Link to full system information report as [Gist](https://gist.github.com/): https://gist.github.com/gbendou/c40a0a6ba29824c196eca0ff84778824
- Proton version: 10.0-2

## I confirm:
- [x] that I haven't found an existing compatibility report for this game.
- [x] that I have checked whether there are updates for my system available.

<!-- Please add `PROTON_LOG=1 %command%` to the game's launch options and
attach the generated $HOME/steam-$APPID.log to this issue report as a file.
(Proton logs compress well if needed.)-->

## Symptoms <!-- What's the problem? --> 
Game worked perfectly on Proton 10.0-1. Once updated to 10.0-2, I began to experience incessant and significant slowdowns and jittering. Hotfix was marginally better, Experimental was about the same. I tried 9.0-4 and the game won’t launch no matter what version I use ever since. Rebooting my computer, verifying the files, deleting the prefix folder and reinstalling the game didn’t help.


## Reproduction
Try switching Proton versions, I guess.

[steam-2513280.log](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/21862117/steam-2513280.log)

following_discussion.discussion_body: @gbendou couldn't get the game to break on my end, but something that caught my attention in your log is the mention of d3d11.dll and d3d11.dll.config in the game's installation folder when these aren't part of the game. If you remove them, does the game work again?

@simifor Aw, man, that's exactly what it was. You're the best, thank you so much! 

Sorry for the wrongful report, that was careless of me.

## I confirm:
- [ x] that I haven't found an existing compatibility report for this game.
- [x ] that I have checked whether there are updates for my system available.



## Symptoms
Throughout the game, it tries to mess with the player by taking non steam screenshots and saving them in what would be the document folder. Under proton however, it fails to save anything in the games compatdata folder. The game also suffers from stutters and freezes, which can last from a few seconds to the point where you have to restart the game itself.

## Reproduction
Set proton version, play through the game until you see what looks to be a filepath in the top left of the screen and the steam screenshot sound to play. then check the compatdata folder for that filepath at /home/LINUXUSER/.steam/steam/steamapps/compatdata/3658110/pfx/drive_c/users/steamuser/Documents/ and notice there is no screenshots or anything of the sort.

following_discussion.discussion_body:
Hello @Dal1ster, `[...]/steamapps/compatdata/3658110` isn't a sane appid in this context. That should be the appid of Proton itself and not the game's wineprefix. Also, 3659070 is the game's demo, which is fine by itself, but note that you've made it ambiguous if you tested the full game or the demo. I'm forced to assume it's the latter.

Please add [`PROTON_LOG=1 %command%`](https://github.com/ValveSoftware/Proton#runtime-config-options) to the game's launch options and attach the generated $HOME/steam-$APPID.log to this issue report as a file. (Proton logs compress well if needed.)

Apologies about the ambiguity, i just tried to follow what the guide suggested, and fill in the title and such with what the template recommends (since the games demo is the Vegetable patch, the main game is just giggleland full stop, but calling it the demo would cause confusion). Also odd that i didnt upload the proton log....  Heres the log then. Also i was just trying to follow my hunch of it saving its own screenshots in the compatdata folder for itself since thats what happens with most proton games I've tested.

[steam-3659070.tar.gz](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/20847411/steam-3659070.tar.gz)

## System Information
- GPU: AMD ATI Radeon 5700 XT
- Driver/LLVM version: NAVI10, DRM 3.42.0, 5.15.6-gentoo, LLVM 13.0.0, 4.6 (Compatibility Profile) Mesa 21.3.1
- Kernel version: 5.15.6-gentoo

- Proton version: experimental-6.3-20211209

## I confirm:
- [x] that I haven't found an existing compatibility report for this game.
- [x] that I have checked whether there are updates for my system available.

<!-- Please add `PROTON_LOG=1 %command%` to the game's launch options and
attach the generated $HOME/steam-$APPID.log to this issue report as a file.
(Proton logs compress well if needed.)-->

## Symptoms <!-- What's the problem? -->
When starting a flight (press the "Fly" button) the loading process starts and runs for a while. Suddenly the game crashes to Desktop. In the console I can see the following error:
`Unhandled exception: unimplemented function api-ms-win-crt-runtime-l1-1-0.dll.feclearexcept called in 64-bit code (0x000000007b0123be).`

[steam-1250410.log.tar.gz](https://github.com/ValveSoftware/Proton/files/7690312/steam-1250410.log.tar.gz)

## Reproduction
1. Install the A32NX extension for MSFS
2. Select the new Aircraft
3. Start the flight by clicking on the "Fly" button.

Using one issue report per game title on the Proton Issue tracker is an exception, rather than a general rule due to the large number of games the Proton devs get feedback on, and there are several bug trackers that the developers need to use on a regular basis. The first half of your question is correct for this issue tracker, #3931 is the right place for all feedback related to running Sea of Thieves with Proton, combined.

compatibility_report:
# Compatibility Report
- Name of the game with compatibility issues: Creatures Docking Station
- Steam AppID of the game: 1659050

## System Information
- GPU: Intel HD Graphics 4000 (IVB GT2)
- Driver/LLVM version: Mesa 21.2.2
- Kernel version: 5.15.5-76051505-generic
- Link to full system information report as [Gist](https://gist.github.com/): https://gist.github.com/truboxl/d89d8496d53cea55cb358acaa5c00ac2
- Proton version: Proton Experimental

## I confirm:
- [x] that I haven't found an existing compatibility report for this game.
- [x] that I have checked whether there are updates for my system available.

<!-- Please add `PROTON_LOG=1 %command%` to the game's launch options and
attach the generated $HOME/steam-$APPID.log to this issue report as a file.
(Proton logs compress well if needed.)-->

## Symptoms <!-- What's the problem? -->
Unable to launch the game. Getting the error:

"The game can not continue: Please change your display settings to be 'High Color (16 bit)'"

![Screenshot from 2021-12-20 22-23-09](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/12658589/146782160-a72cc44c-b8d9-4c68-9868-d935b429062f.png)

## Reproduction
Simply launch the game and choose "Play Creatures Docking Station"

## Notes
There simply no easy way to set 16bit color depth in Linux.

Wine suggest we have to fiddle with `/etc/X11/xorg.conf.d/` or `/etc/X11/xorg.conf` and set something along the lines of `DefaultDepth    16`. But that will cause Steam not to start. See https://wiki.winehq.org/FAQ

```
10.4 Graphics
10.4.1 My application complains about being unable to change the resolution or color depth
You generally need to edit the Screen section of your /etc/X11/xorg.conf to support additional color depths and resolutions. There may also be a problem with Xrandr.
```

Another method is to use Xephyr. See https://wiki.winehq.org/256_Color_Mode
So far adding this line to Launch Options works for me:

```
Xephyr :2 -ac -resizeable -screen 1280x720x16 & env DISPLAY=:2 %command%
```
Additionally need to add these registries to `steamapps/compatdata/1659050/pfx/user.reg` or use `protontricks`
to disable window decoration and window manager control
since there is no window manager running in Xephyr.
Running `mutter` or another WM in Xephyr seems to mess things up for me.
```
[Software\\Wine\\X11 Driver] 1639931810
#time=1d7f4f69fbce18e
"Decorated"="N"
"Managed"="N"
```
Going full screen and then back to windowed screen seems to break the game however. Audio still running though. `Alt+Enter` sometimes fixes the problem.

following_discussion.discussion_body:
> The game installed fine and ran great (platinum) with d3dcompiler_47 and win7 until 22.09.22 where i can no longer join any public match

this is the update that broke it: https://store.steampowered.com/news/app/2087030/view/3395173894699741120
after that a friend and I could no longer join matches.

I helped the developer to fix the game and new update is scheduled to land within 24h hours to make the game work for all linux users. ^-^

The linux module for EOS is enabled since 23rd Aug 2022:
![shatterline-linux-eac-module](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/11302521/192392346-6110b702-3272-40b7-a768-5bee3e0c1943.png)

The developer did setup according to the provided documentation in https://partner.steamgames.com/doc/steamdeck/proton#1 and noted the requirement to update the linux module.

There was a bug in the pipeline which caused the public release of the game to miss `Bin/CrashRpt1403.dll` which was triggering the EOS resulting in linux players being unable to join any public game lobby the solution is to provide the file. Discovered through searching in `Steam/steamapps/compatdata/2087030/pfx/drive_c/users/steamuser/Saved Games/Shatterline/somerandomchars/user/log/Game.log` which included:

> <2022-09-26T22:09:54:789+02> [Warning] (Game) - [EOSCore_Windows::EOSSDKLoggingCallback] LogEOSAntiCheat: [AntiCheatClient] [PollStatusInternal] Client Violation with Type: 5 Message: Unknown file version (Bin/CrashRpt1403.dll), len: 44

The `d3dcompiler_47.dll` **was required** as they broke shaders for proton which will be fixed after the game realease and that currently results in a black textures when you try to join the game, with new update that dependency is provided by upstream so **it's no longer required to workaround it** and workarounds will trigger EOS.

The `win7` workaround is still needed as the game is using **Winsock Registered Input/Output (RIO) API** that is not implemented in wine/proton if the windows release is set on 8 and higher, developer plans to address it before the game gets released or shortly after by replacing it with proton-friendly alternative.

The game is made by former crytek developers who worked on the cryengine so for the game they are using lumberyard fork which currently doesn't support linux exports. The developer is working on porting the renderer and libraries to get steam deck certified and native linux version with it after the game's release.

Relevant discussion on:
* shatterline discord with the devs https://discord.com/channels/750704373113552976/1022511212661067857 
* with frogs https://discord.com/channels/853130811581530142/855613414584221706/1024017540789305414

Proposed long term solution for issues alike is to fix vkd3d-shader used in the d3dcompiler in wine to compile HLSL shaders without the need to use 3rd party d3dcompiler_47 and mentioned RIO API.

---

If you like the game then consider buying the 5 USD/EUR trinket in the game to support the development and work on linux port and proton compatibility

> If you like the game then consider buying the 5 USD/EUR trinket in the game to support the development and work on linux port and proton compatibility

I would but no in-game purchases because the Steam overlay only appears on the EAC launcher window and is not present ingame. Same bug as in Elden Ring.

(Game is indeed working fine though after switching the prefix to win7)

reportedly the solution is to use `gamescope` or worst case scenario use GeForceNOW to buy things in the game

If anyone has any relevant info then let me know

@FirephoenixX02 I noticed that too, from looking at it seems to be caused by game compiling shaders so the issue should fix itself the more you play?

On my Arch Linux system, starting the game gives the Shatterline splash screen, and after a while a warning message pops up:

![Shatterline-Screenshot_2023-03-05_15-40-38](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/929763/222967894-192b86b4-2bc4-448b-a3df-a7556dac664e.png)

Pressing OK closes the window, and while I see the game process is still running, the game never actually starts. Video card is a Radeon RX 6700 XT.

**Steam System Information**: https://gist.github.com/chtaube/db16ccae1068e42d869e5949c930c862

Proton LOG files:
- with Proton experimental: https://gist.github.com/chtaube/9eceee0b4f441c57e8e95243babf3319
- with GE-Proton 7.49: https://gist.github.com/chtaube/416db6cccf11cc38793abd0a787ed4b3

@chtaube the same problem seems to exist with some windows users aswell, so it's likely not related to proton. However I did a forum post where they fixed it by setting compatibility to Windows 8 (https://forums.newworld.com/t/unsupported-video-card-detected/261298/3 New World also uses the Lumberyard engine so results should be the same)

Try using Protontricks to set the windows version to 8 or higher and see if that works.

Hello @chtaube, `info:    Device name:     : Null hardware (RADV NAVI10)` in your Proton log might be related to the all zero vendor / device id in the error message.

Ignoring that, https://gist.github.com/chtaube/db16ccae1068e42d869e5949c930c862#file-gistfile1-txt-L299 / https://gist.github.com/chtaube/db16ccae1068e42d869e5949c930c862#file-gistfile1-txt-L748-L754 and `info:    Driver: 2.0.249` (https://gist.github.com/chtaube/9eceee0b4f441c57e8e95243babf3319#file-steam-2087030-experimental-log-L1010) tells us that the game is using 32 bit AMDVLK. Can you check how the game behaves with your system configured to use 32 bit mesa/RADV?

@kisak-valve Ok, I found the culprit: _amdvlk_ was not installed (anymore?) on my system. Installed the package and restarted Steam and now Shatterline is running. Did a quick check and multiplayer seems to be working, too.

Thanks for pointing me into the right direction.
@ZaxLofful This regression is fixed with experimental-8.0-20230505 :)
@alasky17 : Is there a link to the PR that fixed it and what the problem was?
@ZaxLofful Commit that fixed the problem was https://github.com/ValveSoftware/wine/commit/7035bf12c55228dd57a3787132f3c34654128871

Since some recent updates, the game got broken and the Shatterline.exe binary couldn't found by the anticheat. Finally there's a workaround: install it in the same partition as the Steam files (and the EAC Runtime). Got it working with Proton Experimental (bleeding edge).

I'm experiencing issues when i switched to Proton Experimental: i cannot play the Expedition game mode: the loading screen shows twice, then i transferred back to the main menu.The other game mdoes are working as expected.
With Proton 7.0.2 everything is fine.

Hello @kivimango, please add [`PROTON_LOG=1 %command%`](https://github.com/ValveSoftware/Proton#runtime-config-options) to the game's launch options and attach the generated $HOME/steam-$APPID.log to this issue report as a file. (Proton logs compress well if needed.) Also, please copy your system information from Steam (`Steam` -> `Help` -> `System Information`) and put it in a [gist](https://gist.github.com/), then include a link to the gist in this issue report.

A Proton dev might also be interested in how the game behaves with Proton 8.0.

Not sure if others have similar issue, but my game always crashes 2nd loading of map - pve/pvp. Run a game, works without issue, end the run and re-queue and always crashes while loading the next map.

Same with GE, 8.0.4 and experimental (bleeding-edge).

[steam-2087030.log](https://github.com/ValveSoftware/Proton/files/13744417/steam-2087030.log)

[steam-2087030_exp.log](https://github.com/ValveSoftware/Proton/files/13744628/steam-2087030_exp.log)

> Not sure if others have similar issue, but my game always crashes 2nd loading of map - pve/pvp. Run a game, works without issue, end the run and re-queue and always crashes while loading the next map.
> 
> Same with GE, 8.0.4 and experimental (bleeding-edge).
> 
> [steam-2087030.log](https://github.com/ValveSoftware/Proton/files/13744417/steam-2087030.log)
> 
> [steam-2087030_exp.log](https://github.com/ValveSoftware/Proton/files/13744628/steam-2087030_exp.log)

Yes, this is happening to me as well.  It has been happening for several months now
@spaghetticodez This should now be fixed in the latest Proton Experimental release.
Trying to join a match results in "kicked by anticheat".

> Trying to join a match results in "kicked by anticheat". -- @lordcirth (https://github.com/ValveSoftware/Proton/issues/6165#issuecomment-2185315719)

Try to verify the game's integrity in steam and if it doesn't work then review https://github.com/ValveSoftware/Proton/issues/6165#issuecomment-1258731897 and give us compatibility report with the log from EOS, it should look like this when there is an inconsistency with the game directory:

> <2022-09-26T22:09:54:789+02> [Warning] (Game) - [EOSCore_Windows::EOSSDKLoggingCallback] LogEOSAntiCheat: [AntiCheatClient] [PollStatusInternal] Client Violation with Type: 5 Message: Unknown file version (Bin/CrashRpt1403.dll), len: 44
@lordcirth Joining matches is now working for me.  Could you please retest and see if you still get a message about anti-cheat?

> @lordcirth Joining matches is now working for me. Could you please retest and see if you still get a message about anti-cheat?

Sorry, didn't see this until now (way too many notifications) and the game is dead now. Thanks.

>[...] and the game is dead now. Thanks. -- @lordcirth (https://github.com/ValveSoftware/Proton/issues/6165#issuecomment-2467191642)

It's not dead, they are moving from Early Access/Beta Phase to Full Release as they finally decided on which publisher and monetization model to use.

![image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/e101fd76-cc28-4b5a-9769-55e88871e677)

The game won't let you into an online match unless it's able to verify the signatures of **ALL** game files which is likely your case (thus the suggestion in https://github.com/ValveSoftware/Proton/issues/6165#issuecomment-2186014531) as either you or your system must have changed something for the anti-cheat to fail it's checks -> Verify the game integrity and it should work again.

Note that on Linux under proton the game has much stricter anti-cheat than on Windows as the Linux's design enables for a deeper integration with the OS and the kernel. On Windows it doesn't seem to care as much about file integrity and if you prefer to play via Epic Games Store then there is an option of https://heroicgameslauncher.com which is an open-source launcher made by the community which should handle these things for you as well as Steam.

Relevant to the issue: To show that the game is not dead with ongoing development to support Linux.
@Kreyren It is dead on Linux. They moved to egs and does not support anti-cheat on Linux anymore. There is no single clue they are gonna support Linux.

Yeah I've tried to get it to work, it runs very well with heroic launcher (much better than on Windows actually), even epic online services work, but joining an online match gets me kicked by anticheat in seconds...
If anyone has a working setup, please let me know.

I talked with the developer and they told me the following:

![image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/13e2c013-4e88-4769-934c-db7a777b6724)

The Linux Anti-Cheat Module is active and was never disabled. The EAC Driver initialization returns fail when running under Heroic as it is not responding on the client side for server EAC request which leads to the kick for them to conclude that this is not an error on their side as they used the same code before and on Steam deck. It seems that using EGS as user base platform blocks it from using EAC.

CC @kisak-valve Could you look into this?

@Kreyren Honestly I do not think Valve would be interested in spending time on this issue. The game is not on Steam anymore. Proton is a fork of wine designed to work specifically with the Steam. Therefore this issue is out of scope for Proton and Valve devs. Why would they care whether EGS exclusive is working on Linux or not?

From what i understood for Valve it's important that games and everything works in general on Linux due to the existential threat of Xbox/Microsoft Store for this to be in the scope while the devs still have steam developer account and i assume will most likely release the game on steam later assuming the trend with other EGS exclusives e.g. Fall Guys due to the publisher's contract to finance the game's development and marketing.

Plus afaik from the developer's side this is pre-defined way in lumberyard to enable anti-cheat so addressing this will likely be beneficial for other lumberyard games and their linux support.

Noting that the problem here appears to be in the anti-cheat itself that the community doesn't have access in comparison to valve who previously fixed very similar issue.

following_discussion.discussion_body: Hello @xcom169, we're using one issue report per unofficially supported game title, so I've gone ahead and transferred this issue report to https://github.com/ValveSoftware/Proton/issues/2386#issuecomment-873401999.
